data.title: Trump: Putin will respond to Ukraine's drone attack, immediate peace unlikely
data.description:
President Donald Trump said he spoke to Vladimir Putin by phone for more than an hour, during which the Russian leader said he will respond to Ukraine's recent drone attack.

"It was a good conversation, but not a conversation that will lead to immediate peace," Trump posted on Truth Social. "President Putin did say, and very strongly, that he will have to respond to the recent attack on the airfields."

The leaders also discussed Iran nuclear talks. "Putin suggested that he will participate in the discussions with Iran and that he could, perhaps, be helpful in getting this brought to a rapid conclusion," Trump said. 

The Kremlin confirmed the call, but did not disclose what Putin told Trump about Russia's plans to respond to what it said were "terrorist attacks" by Ukraine.

Ukraine's large-scale drone strike on Sunday targeted Russian air bases hosting nuclear-capable long-range bombers. On Saturday, two bridges in Russian regions bordering Ukraine collapsed, but Kyiv did not claim responsibility for this.

"Many have spoken with Russia at various levels," Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. "But none of these talks have brought a reliable peace, or even stopped the war. If the powerful do not stop Putin, it means they share responsibility with him. And if they want to stop him but cannot, then Putin will no longer see them as powerful."

After disclosing the call, Trump shared a Washington Post op-ed by conservative author Marc Thiessen about how Congress can provide leverage to coerce Putin to end the war.

"Congress can give it to him by passing the Sanctioning Russia Act," Thiessen wrote, referring to a bipartisan bill that seeks 500% tariffs on any country that buys Russia's energy products, as well as other sanctions.

data.title: Swiss chocolate stocks diverge: Lindt gains, Barry Callebaut struggles amid soaring cocoa prices
data.description:
Swiss chocolate makers are feeling the heat as cocoa prices (CC1:COM) surge to record highs, causing a sharp split in industry performance.

While Lindt & Sprüngli has managed to grow sales and maintain pricing power, rival Barry Callebaut is struggling with declining volumes and rising costs, highlighting the uneven impact of commodity pressures on the sector.

Lindt & Sprüngli AG (OTCPK:LDSVF) (OTCPK:COCXF) has risen 29% to date on SIX Swiss Exchange, as the maker of Lindor has demonstrated its ability to pass on higher costs to customers, aided by the launch of crowd-drawing products such as Dubai-style chocolate. 

Barry Callebaut (OTCPK:BRRLY) (OTCPK:BYCBF), the world's largest cocoa processor, on the other hand, has been particularly affected. The company reported a 0.3% decline in chocolate sales volumes in the third quarter ending in May, with a 7% drop in Eastern Europe. The company’s shares have fallen 29% on weighed down by a lack of pricing power.

Overall, the Swiss chocolate industry is navigating a challenging environment marked by soaring cocoa prices. While larger companies like Lindt appear better positioned to absorb these costs, smaller producers and those with less pricing power may struggle to maintain profitability.

“Barry Callebaut faces a perfect storm of subdued demand and limited pricing power,” said Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Ignacio Canals Polo. By contrast, “Lindt stands out amid the current cocoa market turmoil, leveraging its premium positioning."

Lindt, a key player in the premium chocolate market, has successfully gained market share from rivals like Mondelez International (NASDAQ:MDLZ) One major driver of this growth is the launch of its Dubai-inspired chocolate late last year, which UBS Group AG analyst Joern Iffert called a “blockbuster,” describing it as one of Lindt’s most successful product launches to date.

Meanwhile, Barry Callebaut has cut its sales outlook for the year, sending the stock lower.

data.title: ATIF Holdings unveils five-year plan to acquire 1,000 Bitcoin through purchases and mining
data.description:
ATIF Holdings (NASDAQ:ZBAI) Thursday announced its strategic expansion into the Bitcoin (BTC) sector, with an ambitious five-year plan to accumulate 1,000 BTC through a combination of direct purchases and mining operations.

The company has tentatively selected West Texas as the primary location for its proprietary mining operations.

Additionally, ATIF Holdings (NASDAQ:ZBAI) aims to implement advanced power utilization strategies to further reduce production costs and enhance overall operational efficiency.

data.title: BitFuFu GAAP EPS of -$0.10
data.description:
• BitFuFu press release (NASDAQ:FUFU): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.10.
  • Total revenue was $78.0 million, a decrease of 46.0% from $144.4 million in the same period of 2024. This decrease was primarily attributable to the Bitcoin (“BTC”) mining “halving event” occurred in April 2024, increase in blockchain network difficulty and the temporary decrease in the Company’s hashrate under management year-over-year.
  • Total hashrate decreased by 28.0% to 20.6 EH/s as of March 31, 2025, compared to 28.6 EH/S as of March 31, 2024. This decrease was primarily due to the expiration of certain hash rate purchase orders and miner fleet relocation or upgrades by suppliers.
  • Power capacity was 478 MW as of March 31, 2025, compared to 644 MW as of March 31, 2024.
  • Cloud-mining registered users increased by 89.1% to 607,377 as of March 31, 2025, compared to 321,184 as of March 31, 2024.
  • BTC held by the Company increased by 71.2% to 1,835 BTCs as of March 31, 2025, compared to 1,072 BTCs as of March 31, 2024.

data.title: Yuanbao GAAP EPADS of $0.89, revenue of $133.7M
data.description:
• Yuanbao press release (NASDAQ:YB): Q1 GAAP EPADS of $0.89.
  • Revenue of $133.7M, representing a 43.8% increase from the same period of 2024.
  • Non-GAAP adjusted net income margin in the first quarter of 2025 was 32.2%, compared with 22.8% in the same period of 2024.
  • Net operating cash inflow in the first quarter of 2025 was $58.6 million.
  • Number of new policies in the first quarter of 2025 was 6.8 million, representing a 21.3% increase from 5.6 million in the same period of 2024.

data.title: United Microelectronics’ May revenue declines slightly Y/Y to NT$19.48B
Targa Resources prices $1.5B senior notes
data.description:
• Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) priced an underwritten public offering of $750M aggregate principal amount of its 4.900% senior notes due 2030 and $750M aggregate principal amount of its 5.650% senior notes due 2036 at a price to the public of 99.870% and 99.700% of their face value, respectively.
  • The offering is expected to close on June 18, 2025.
  • The company expects to use a portion of the net proceeds from the offering to redeem the 6.500% senior notes due 2027 issued by Targa Resources Partners LP and to use the remaining net proceeds for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Precious metals climb as silver jumps over 3% amid safe-haven demand
data.description:
Precious metals rose on Thursday, with silver gaining more than 3%, driven by a softer US dollar and demand for safe-haven assets.

Silver prices (XAGUSD:CUR) hovered around $35.46 per ounce on Thursday, while gold (XAUUSD:CUR) was up +0.5% to $3,389.06 an ounce at the time of writing.

The ADP report released Wednesday revealed that the U.S. private sector added just 37,000 jobs in May—falling far short of expectations and marking the weakest gain in over two years.

Meanwhile, the ISM services PMI showed contraction for the first time in nearly a year, with a steep decline in new business and rising input costs, likely driven by newly imposed tariffs.

President Donald Trump, meanwhile, continued to push the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates, though Fed officials have maintained a cautious stance amid ongoing uncertainty. 

On the trade front, sentiment took another hit after Trump said reaching a deal with Chinese President Xi Jinping was “extremely hard,” despite earlier signs the two might speak this week.

Elsewhere, tariff-related growth and demand worries combined with rising OPEC production has recently helped attract increased short-selling interest, which is now being reduced, thereby adding support, Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen said in a note.

"Overall, we see limited upside potentials amid ongoing concerns about a supply glut and softening demand growth," he added.

WTI and Brent crude oil continue to trade within a relatively wide $10 range, established after the sharp selloff in early April.

data.title: Launch day for the Nintendo Switch 2: Here's what to know
data.description:
Gamers have been gearing up for the launch of the Nintendo Switch 2 on Thursday, which is likely to be in short supply due to enormous demand. That's despite a price tag of $450 and premium game titles like Mario Kart World going for $80. The next-generation console, which will be backward-compatible, features a bigger screen and sleeker graphics, as well as ergonomic and magnetic Joy-Cons, with larger buttons and better haptics.

Backdrop: Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOY) scored a major victory with its original Switch console, which hit the market in 2017. Eight years later, the gaming giant has sold more than 150M units of the hybrid system, making the list as the third best-selling game console of all time (behind the PlayStation 2 and Nintendo DS). Any future success will be closely watched by Sony (SONY) and Microsoft (MSFT), which both released their last consoles in 2020 (the PS5 and Xbox Series X/S).

The midnight launch for the Nintendo Switch 2 took place at locations including GameStop (GME) and Best Buy (BBY), with retailers stocking additional supply for customers who weren't able to pre-order. Even before the big event, investors have been responding to the excitement surrounding the console, with ADRs of Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOY) soaring 43% YTD. The enthusiasm has also helped Nintendo reach a market value of close to $100B, becoming the eighth largest publicly traded company in Japan.

SA commentary: "Despite tariff challenges, Nintendo's strategic pricing and accessory adjustments should mitigate impacts, and I expect 17M units to be sold by March 2026," writes SA Analyst Compounding Chef. "Nintendo's FY 2025 sales and profits dropped as Switch 1 ages, but these numbers should not be relevant in the current context," adds Investing Group Leader Best Anchor Stocks, discussing the latest earnings released in May. "FY 2026 guidance seems conservative, but it's normal to see margins drop initially."

data.title: Apple iPhone assembler Foxconn reports 11.92% Y/Y revenue growth in May
data.description:
Foxconn — which is formerly known as Hon Hai Precision (OTCPK:HNHAF) has reported revenue of NT$615.75 billion for May 2025, representing a decrease of 3.99% M/M and an increase of 11.92% Y/Y.

The Taiwan-based company said cumulative revenue for the first five months of 2025 jumped 21.65% year-over-year to NT$2.38T.

Segment break-down:

The revenue performance by product segment shows variations:

Smart Consumer Electronics grew M/M due to strong momentum despite exchange rate issues, was flattish Y/Y.

Cloud and Networking Products declined slightly from last month, and declined Y/Y.

Computing Products also experienced a slight decline M/M, however achieved strong growth Y/Y.

Components and Other Products saw a M/M decline, however revenue delivered significant growth Y/Y.

Q2 Outlook:

The Apple (AAPL) supplier — which is formerly known as Hon Hai Precision said the Cloud and Networking Products segment anticipates continued growth in Q2, despite it being a seasonal off-peak and a product transition period. Current forecasts project both quarter-over-quarter and year-over-year growth. However, the segment will closely monitor the impact of evolving global political and economic conditions, as well as exchange rate fluctuations.
